LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE ROSENTHAL ACT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED THAT DEFENDANT IS A DEBT COLLECTOR AND THE COURT NEEDS TO TAKE THAT ALLEGATION AS TRUE. SECOND PLAINTIFF ALLEGES ILLEGAL DEBT COLLECTION ACTIVITIES AGAINST DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THIS CAUSE OF ACTION REFERS TO SEVERAL DIFFERENT CONTRACTS. PLAINTIFF NEEDS TO BE CLEAR AS TO WHICH CONTRACT APPLIES TO THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE UCL IS OVERRULED. THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IS PREMISED ON A VIOLATION OF THE ROSENTHAL ACT IN THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION WHERE THE DEMURRER WAS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND THE VIOLATION OF CCP 726 UNDER THE UCL CAUSE OF ACTION. IN ADDITION, THE COURT STATED PLAINTIFF HAS 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Jonathan G. Stein, Esq. of Law Offices of Jonathan G. Stein for pltf & opposing party (appearing via CourtCall); Joseph K. Kanada, Esq. of Morrison Foerster 650/813-5925 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's Demurrer to the 1st cause of action for Violation of the Rosenthal Act is overruled. Plaintiff has alleged that Defendant is a debt collector and the Court needs to take that allegation as true. Second Plaintiff alleges illegal debt collection activities against Defendant.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action for Breach of Implied Covenant is sustained with leave to amend. This cause of action refers to several different contracts. Plaintiff needs to be clear as to which contract applies to this cause of action.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 4th cause of action for Violation of the UCL is overruled. This cause of action is premised on a violation of the Rosenthal act in the 1st cause of action where the Demurrer was overruled.
Plaintiff has leave to amend the Violation of CCP 726 under the UCL cause of action. In addition, the Court stated plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-06-2011 Continued To Jul-07-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
